market_direction.summary: Domestic white-collar demand remains uneven, with selective hiring resilience in roles tied to operational continuity.
evidence: Korean hiring momentum appears concentrated in selected functions rather than broad-based expansion.
Career transition outcomes improve when role continuity and timing discipline are preserved.

competition_pressure.summary: Competition remains moderate for senior candidates with adjacent-role portability, but positioning clarity still matters.
evidence: Candidates with directly portable responsibility scope tend to convert faster than broad role-switch applicants.
Role framing around proven delivery reduces competitive drag during mid-cycle transitions.

economic_pressure.summary: Macro uncertainty is still present, creating selective budget discipline rather than full hiring freeze conditions.
evidence: Enterprise hiring pace remains cautious with staggered approvals.
Compensation normalization pressure is visible in non-critical growth tracks.

transition_friction.summary: Transition friction appears manageable when runway and target scope are kept aligned, but delayed pivoting raises strain.
evidence: Longer search windows increase pressure when target role scope is broad or unconstrained.
Structured milestone checks reduce reversible-transition loss.

source_notes: Perplexity Sonar synthesis across labor market and macro updates.
Signals interpreted for AMC structural decision context.
